title: out and ref parameters should be avoided
description: "out" and "ref" parameters should not be usedAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellsuspiciousWhy is this an issue?Passing a parameter by reference, which is what happens when you use theoutorrefparameter modifiers, means that the method will receive a pointer to the argument, rather than the argument itself. If the argument was a value type, the method will be able to change the argument’s values. If it was a reference type, then the method receives a pointer to a pointer, which is usually not what was intended.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Method uses ref or out parameters - consider returning values instead for better clarity

logic.detection_conditions: Uses regex to detect ref or out parameters in method declarations
logic.triggering_conditions: Method declarations containing ref or out parameter modifiers
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes test methods, P/Invoke, TryParse patterns, performance-critical types, and logging contexts
logic.reasoning: Detects ref and out parameters which can make code harder to understand and maintain. However, excludes common idiomatic patterns like TryParse, P/Invoke declarations, and performance-critical scenarios where ref/out are beneficial.

should_trigger: public void Process(ref int input, out bool success)
should_not_trigger: public bool TryParseCustom(string input, out int result)
[DllImport] public static extern bool GetWindowRect(IntPtr hWnd, out RECT lpRect)
